visualSignals: Face value, coupon rate, and yield rate given
verbalTriggers: What should an investor pay for this bond

decisionTree.question: Are you finding price given yield?
decisionTree.ifNo: For yield given price: trial and error

steps.instruction: Identify face value (F), coupon rate (c), yield rate (i), years to maturity (n)
steps.pitfall: Confusing coupon rate with yield rate
steps.check: Coupon rate determines payments; yield rate is discount rate

steps.instruction: Calculate coupon payment: Coupon = F × c / payments_per_year
steps.check: Semi-annual: divide annual coupon rate by 2

steps.instruction: Set up bond price formula: P = Coupon(P/A,i,n) + Face(P/F,i,n)

steps.pitfall: Using nominal annual rate instead of periodic rate
steps.check: i should be rate per coupon period

commonErrors.symptom: Bond price > face value when yield > coupon rate
commonErrors.diagnosis: Using wrong rates - price and yield are inversely related
commonErrors.fix: If yield > coupon, bond sells at discount (price < face)
For semi-annual: n×2 periods, i/2 per period, coupon/2 per payment

workedExamples.problem: A 10-year bond with $10,000 face value has 8% coupon paid quarterly. Find price for 12% nominal yield.

solution.math: F = 10,000, c = 8%/4 = 2% per quarter, i = 12%/4 = 3% per quarter, n = 10×4 = 40

solution.explanation: Bond sells at discount since yield > coupon

workedExamples.keyInsight: Yield > Coupon → Price < Face Value (discount bond)

questions.integral: Face value, coupon rate, yield rate, time to maturity
questions.explanation: These are the key bond pricing parameters

problems.problem: $1000 face, 6% coupon paid semi-annually, 5 years, yield 8% nominal. Find price.
5-year bond, $1000 face, 10% coupon semi-annual, investor wants 12% return. Maximum price?

selfCheckQuestions: Did I adjust for semi-annual coupons?
Is yield rate different from coupon rate?
